relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Promises invite cleansing and holiness completed in reverence.
Paul asks them to make room in the heart.
This is not condemnation; they are in his heart to die and live together.
Boldness, boasting, comfort, and joy overflow inside pressure.
Macedonia brings no bodily rest: conflicts outside, fears within.
The Divine comforts the lowly through Titus’ arrival.
Titus brings news of longing, mourning, and zeal.
The painful letter grieved them for a season.
Paul rejoices not in grief itself but grief that turned them toward life.
Divine grief works turning into saving/healing; worldly grief works death.
Their grief produced earnestness, repair, longing, zeal, and correction.
The letter revealed their earnestness before the Divine.
Their response comforted Paul and refreshed Titus.
Paul’s boast about them became truth.
Titus’ affection deepens as he remembers their reverent welcome.
Paul rejoices in confidence toward them.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 7 distinguishes healing grief from death-grief. The point is not shame but turning, repair, and renewed trust.
